records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sidetracked (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Sidetracking.]

defs: To transfer to a siding from a main line of track.

Hence, fig., to divert or reduce to a position or condition that is relatively secondary or subordinate in activity, importance, effectiveness, or the like; to switch off; to turn aside, as from a purpose.
quotes: Such a project was, in fact, sidetracked in favor of the census of school children.
